channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Air France and Airbus fined for 2009 crash that killed 228 people
item.description: France's national airline Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash that killed 228 people.

media:group.media:description: Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash.

item.title: Another group of Australians with IS-links leave camp in Syria for Australia
item.description: The ABC has seen buses carrying the group which left the Al Roj camp on Thursday afternoon local time.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: A bus carrying Australian women and children left the Al Roj camp on Thursday afternoon, local time.

item.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel and Memorial Days triumph at book awards
item.description: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story

media:group.media:description: Three respected authors — Sarah Krasnostein, Helen Garner and Chloe Hooper — joined forces to write the now-award-winning The Mushroom Tapes.

item.title: Pauline Hanson caught swearing as staffer says 'shut up' to journalist
item.description: Pauline Hanson and her party have again been caught in tense moments with media, with the One Nation leader captured on camera swearing and a staffer telling a journalist to "shut up".

media:group.media:description: One Nation Leader Pauline Hanson was in Adelaide for an oil and gas conference.

item.title: Police arrest man after alleged threats to carry out Bourke St attack
item.description: A man who allegedly threatened to carry out an incident in Melbourne's Bourke Street tonight has been arrested by police.

media:group.media:description: Victoria Police on patrol in Melbourne.

item.title: Nampijinpa Price: 'Extremist ideology' destroying Australian culture
item.description: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price has denied that she agreed with a podcaster saying Australia should stop 'flooding' Australia with migrants from Asia, Africa and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong and Sara Tomevska

media:group.media:description: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price says if people want to come to Australia they have to "adopt our values".

item.title: Queen was 'very keen' her son Andrew got UK trade job, documents reveal
item.description: The historic documents show no security vetting took place at the time.

media:group.media:description: Former prince Andrew served as the UK's Special Representative for International Trade and Investment.

item.title: Toxic bait spread closes school in mouse-plagued Morawa
item.description: Students of the Western Australian School of Agriculture in Morawa were sent home and the school shut after toxic mouse bait intended for paddocks was spread on site.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Prendergast and Chris Lewis

media:group.media:description: The Western Australian College of Agriculture in Morawa is closed after the mouse bait was spread on school grounds.

item.title: Father of flotilla detainee makes serious allegations against Israel during live interview
item.description: Israel's ambassador to Australia Hillel Newman has stopped short of apologising to the Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained in Israel – who had been taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.
item.dc:creator: Paul Johnson

media:group.media:description: Ethan Floyd, Neve O'Connor and Zack Schofield in a still from their video message.

item.title: 'I've never been so confused': Raiders slam officials, fall to Dolphins in thrilling Canberra clash
item.description: The Raiders huffed and puffed but couldn't overcome the depleted Dolphins, who despite missing six key players through injury and Origin won a fourth-straight NRL game for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Ricky Stuart said he has never been so confused about officiating.

item.title: 'Hard to achieve, in our opinion': Ratings agency unimpressed with Tas budget
item.description: Credit rating agency S&P Global says Tasmania's budget repair ambitions face "high execution risk". The government aims to save $1.5 billion through cuts.
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff will need to work hard to sell the budget to some stakeholders.

item.title: Hanson proposes tax break for drilling alongside new gas royalty
item.description: The One Nation leader wants a flat royalty on the production of new gas projects.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Pauline Hanson says the policy would ensure taxpayers receive a fairer return on natural resources.

item.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tasmania farewell by claiming Crows thriller
item.description: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Blake Hardwick kicked four goals including the match-sealer with two minutes left as Hawthorn claimed a nine-point win over Adelaide.

item.title: Man charged with five counts of rape following alleged Adelaide Hills break-in
item.description: A man accused of breaking into an Adelaide Hills home and sexually assaulting a person unknown to him has been charged with multiple counts of rape, court documents show.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: The 27-year-old accused appeared in the Adelaide Magistrates Court.

item.title: Elon Musk could become world's first trillionaire as SpaceX files for largest IPO ever
item.description: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: SpaceX currently has deals for 28 planned National Security Space Launch missions.

item.title: Court hears former AFL footballer Nick Stevens likely to face prison term
item.description: A Victorian court hears former AFL player Nick Stevens was a blue-collar worker but committed a white-collar crime, ahead of his sentencing for defrauding six families in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Wade Stephens

media:group.media:description: Ex-AFL player Nick Stevens appeared in prison greens during a County Court hearing on Thursday.

item.title: Olympics minister stands aside after electoral commission makes referral to police
item.description: Queensland's Olympics Minister Tim Mander has stood aside from cabinet after he was referred to police by the Australian Electoral Commission.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Tim Mander says he has told Premier David Crisafulli he is "standing aside" while the matter referred to the Australian Federal Police is "being sorted".

item.title: Albanese's hard sell as budget opponents line up
item.description: For a group of people who declared they were prepared to tackle a post-budget scare campaign, the government has looked anything but in control this week.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers have been on the post-budget sell. It hasn't been easy.

item.title: 'Will be a deadline': Wagga homeless community urged to take new offer
item.description: The NSW minister for homelessness says people will be offered additional support to move out of camps around Wagga Wagga, but that they will be forced to relocate eventually.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Pezet

media:group.media:description: Rose Jackson visited Wagga Wagga to speak with the emergency cross-agency taskforce.

item.title: Drug testing of athletes to expand as influencers fuel illicit peptides boom
item.description: The nation's anti-doping agency reveals its plans to expand drug-testing in sport in response to the soaring popularity of unregulated peptides in the broader community.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Willan

media:group.media:description: Casey Wesche purchased unregulated peptides online to help with weight loss.

item.title: Man with 'very serious' criminal history jailed over motorcyclist's death
item.description: The man had a "very high" level of methamphetamine in his blood when he crashed into the back of Ian Welldon's motorbike while failing to stop at a red light.
item.dc:creator: Dan McCray

media:group.media:description: Allan Eveleigh will spend the next 10 months behind bars before being eligible for parole.

item.title: Blues fighting for an Origin dynasty a decade in the making
item.description: Ten years after playing in New South Wales's breakthrough victory over Queensland, Isabelle Kelly returns to the Gold Coast on the cusp of launching a Blues Origin dynasty.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Isabelle Kelly will captain New South Wales in next Thursday's Origin finale.

item.title: Support worker cut keys to NDIS participant's home without knowledge, inquiry hears
item.description: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.
item.dc:creator: Evan Young

media:group.media:description: Concerns have been raised at a parliamentary hearing into the NDIS about how complaints are handled.

item.title: What you need to know about tomorrow's ACT public school strike
item.description: For the first time in 15 years, ACT public school staff are going on strike in a two-hour work stoppage that will see all public schools, including specialist schools and preschools, keep their doors closed until 10:30am on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: ACT public schools will be closed for two hours on Friday morning, resulting in a later start to the school day.

item.title: Public transport to remain free in Tasmania for an additional year
item.description: The Tasmanian government has committed $24 million in the state budget towards keeping buses and ferries free for one more year.
item.dc:creator: Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: Premier Jeremy Rockliff says keeping public transport free was a "significant" but "worthy" investment.
